title: Why I traded my spreadsheets for a custom PostgreSQL dashboard
author: Disastrous_Hotel_574
selftext:
Hi everyone, as a developer, I realized my biggest trading losses weren't from bad strategy, but from data fatigue and emotional execution. I was tired of Excel freezing during high-volatility spikes on Gold, so I built my own infrastructure to handle the noise.

The project is called Hercul Market, built with Next.js and Supabase to move away from manual logging and toward algorithmic discipline.

[https://hercul-market.vercel.app](https://hercul-market.vercel.app)

How it changed my execution this week:

* Automated Structure: The system uses AI to map out Order Blocks and liquidity zones, stripping away my own human bias.
* Precision Results: I managed to close a 5.5 RR trade for a 1,200 profit by trusting the data instead of my gut.
* Real-time Analytics: My XIRR and drawdown are calculated instantly in the backend, giving me a cold, hard look at my performance.
* No More "Psychology" Issues: By hardcoding my risk parameters, I stopped second-guessing every 1m candle and let the math work.

The tool is in free beta for anyone who wants to stop "hoping" and start tracking with engineering-level rigor. Link is at the top.

Are you guys still using manual journals, or have you moved to a more automated stack for your performance tracking? 📈💻

comments.author: Opening_Kitchen_5349
comments.body:
I’ve tried manual journaling before, but it takes a lot of time and things get missed.

Now I'm using the SuperTrader journal app because it’s more affordable than others.

comments.author: Front-Audience-4045
comments.body:
The framing is spot on — most traders optimize their strategy and completely ignore their execution infrastructure, which is where the edge actually gets lost.



Automating Order Block detection and wiring it directly into your risk parameters is a meaningful architectural decision, not just a convenience feature. That 5.5 RR close is a direct product of removing the human in the loop at the right moment.



I develop AI-powered trading systems and have built similar infrastructure from the execution layer up. Projects like this are where the most interesting problems live — happy to dig into it further if you're looking to push the system to the next level.

comments.author: dogazine4570
comments.body:
Love this angle. A lot of people blame “strategy” when it’s really execution fatigue and tool friction. Excel freezing during volatility spikes is more than annoying — it actually changes decision timing, which compounds emotionally.

Curious about a few things:

- Are you pulling live tick data into Supabase, or batching at intervals?
- Did you notice measurable improvement in metrics (reduced overtrading, better R:R, lower variance), or was it mostly psychological discipline?
- How are you handling latency and failover during high-volume events?

One thing I’ve found when moving from spreadsheets to a custom dashboard is that automation can reduce emotional noise, but it can also hide data context if not designed carefully. How are you balancing clean UI vs. keeping enough raw detail visible?

Cool project overall — building your own infrastructure forces clarity in your process.

replies.author: Disastrous_Hotel_574
replies.body:
You hit the nail on the head—**execution fatigue** is the silent account killer. Moving from spreadsheets to a custom dashboard was exactly about solving that "tool friction" so we can focus on the trade, not the tech.

To answer your technical and performance questions:

* **Data Handling:** We use a hybrid approach. We pull live tick data for high-volatility assets like **BTC, NVIDIA, and Gold**, while utilizing optimized batching for our broader dashboard metrics to keep the UI snappy. This helps us avoid the "Excel freeze" you mentioned during the 9:30 AM EST spikes.
* **Performance Metrics:** The biggest shift hasn't been a "magic" increase in win rate, but a massive **reduction in variance** and **overtrading**. By having the bias pre-mapped at the US open, the "psychological discipline" leads to better R:R because we aren't chasing candles out of FOMO.
* **Latency & Failover:** We’ve built the infrastructure with redundancy in mind to ensure the daily briefing is live exactly at **15:30 CET**.
* **UI vs. Context:** This is the constant battle. Our philosophy for **HerculMarket** is "High Signal, Low Noise." We hide the raw complexity behind a clean interface but keep the key institutional levels (liquidity/OBs) front and center so the context is never lost.

The Beta ends this **Monday, March 23**, and we’re moving to the **€15/month** model to keep scaling this infrastructure. Would love to have a seasoned eye like yours check out the final version:[https://herculmarket.com/](https://herculmarket.com/)
